title: Hadoop MapReduce Intermediate Directory
description:
This directory contains several Java files that play crucial roles in the Hadoop MapReduce framework. The `ClusterMetrics.java` file defines a class for tracking cluster metrics, while `ContextFactory.java` offers a factory for creating MapReduce Context Objects. `CryptoUtils.java` handles cryptographic streams for encrypted intermediate data, and `CustomJobEndNotifier.java` allows for custom job end notifications. 

The `MRJobConfig.java` file defines constants for configuring MapReduce jobs, and `MapContext.java` provides context to the Mapper. `MarkableIterator.java` and `MarkableIteratorInterface.java` support mark-reset functionality in Hadoop MapReduce. `OutputCommitter.java` and `OutputFormat.java` manage task output commit and output specifications, respectively. 

`QueueAclsInfo.java` and `QueueInfo.java` handle job queue Access Control Lists (ACLs) and information, while `RecordReader.java` defines the abstract RecordReader class for reading input data. These components collectively ensure seamless job execution and management in the MapReduce framework.
summary:
TITLE: Hadoop MapReduce Intermediate Directory
DESCRIPTION: This directory contains several Java files that play crucial roles in the Hadoop MapReduce framework. The `ClusterMetrics.java` file defines a class for tracking cluster metrics, while `ContextFactory.java` offers a factory for creating MapReduce Context Objects. `CryptoUtils.java` handles cryptographic streams for encrypted intermediate data, and `CustomJobEndNotifier.java` allows for custom job end notifications. 

The `MRJobConfig.java` file defines constants for configuring MapReduce jobs, and `MapContext.java` provides context to the Mapper. `MarkableIterator.java` and `MarkableIteratorInterface.java` support mark-reset functionality in Hadoop MapReduce. `OutputCommitter.java` and `OutputFormat.java` manage task output commit and output specifications, respectively. 

`QueueAclsInfo.java` and `QueueInfo.java` handle job queue Access Control Lists (ACLs) and information, while `RecordReader.java` defines the abstract RecordReader class for reading input data. These components collectively ensure seamless job execution and management in the MapReduce framework.

intermediate_summaries.name: . chunk 0
intermediate_summaries.summary:
The `ClusterMetrics.java` file defines a `ClusterMetrics` class that encapsulates various metrics of a MapReduce cluster, such as running tasks, occupied slots, and job submissions. It implements the `Writable` interface for serialization, utilizing dependencies like `java.io.DataInput`, `java.io.DataOutput`, and `java.io.IOException`.

`ContextFactory.java` provides a factory for creating MapReduce Context Objects, handling version inconsistencies between Hadoop versions. It initializes static constructors and fields for different context types, offering methods to clone contexts with new configurations. Key dependencies include `org.apache.hadoop.conf.Configuration`, `org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.JobID`, and `org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.TaskAttemptID`.

`CryptoUtils.java` offers utilities for handling cryptographic streams in MapReduce, particularly for encrypted intermediate data. It uses the `CryptoCodec` class and depends on Hadoop's file system and security classes.

`CustomJobEndNotifier.java` defines an interface for custom job end notifiers, allowing users to implement their own notification mechanisms beyond Hadoop's built-in notifier. It depends on `org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.Configuration` and `MRJobConfig` for job configuration properties.

These components collectively contribute to the MapReduce framework's functionality, with each focusing on specific responsibilities while collaborating to ensure seamless job execution and management.

intermediate_summaries.name: . chunk 1
intermediate_summaries.summary:
The `MRJobConfig.java` file defines constants for configuring Apache Hadoop MapReduce jobs, including input/output formats, map/reduce classes, and resource management settings. It relies on Hadoop's `MRJobConfig` and `org.apache.hadoop.classification` packages.

`MapContext.java` introduces the `MapContext` interface, extending `TaskInputOutputContext`, to provide context to the `Mapper`. It includes the `getInputSplit()` method, returning the `InputSplit` for the map task, which represents the logical partition of input data.

`MarkableIterator.java` presents a `MarkableIterator` class, wrapping an existing iterator to support mark, reset, and clear operations, while maintaining standard iterator methods. It depends on Hadoop's `MarkableIteratorInterface` and standard Java classes.

`MarkableIteratorInterface.java` defines the `MarkableIteratorInterface`, specifying `mark()`, `reset()`, and `clearMark()` methods for iterators supporting mark-reset functionality in Hadoop MapReduce. It depends on `TaskInputOutputContext`, `IOException`, `Iterator`, and Hadoop classification annotations.

`OutputCommitter.java` outlines the `OutputCommitter` abstract class for managing task output commit in MapReduce jobs, with methods for setup, cleanup, commit, and abort of job and task outputs. It depends on `JobContext`, `TaskAttemptContext`, `JobStatus.State`, and Hadoop classification annotations.

`OutputFormat.java` defines the `OutputFormat` abstract class for specifying output specifications of MapReduce jobs, providing a `RecordWriter` for output files and managing output committers. It depends on `JobContext`, `TaskAttemptContext`, `FileSystem`, and Hadoop classification annotations.

`QueueAclsInfo.java` encapsulates Access Control Lists (ACLs) for a specific job queue in Hadoop MapReduce, storing the queue name and allowed operations. It depends on `DataInput`, `DataOutput`, `Text`, `Writable`, `WritableUtils`, and `StringInterner`.

`QueueInfo.java` stores and manages information about job queues, including name, scheduling info, state, job statuses, and child queues. It depends on `Text`, `Writable`, `WritableUtils`, `Properties`, and standard Java classes.

`RecordReader.java` defines the abstract `RecordReader` class for reading key-value pairs from input data in MapReduce jobs, with methods for initialization

intermediate_summaries.name: . chunk 2
intermediate_summaries.summary: The 'TaskCounter.java' file defines an enumeration, `TaskCounter`, within the `org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce` package, providing constants for tracking various metrics during Hadoop MapReduce tasks. The 'TaskInputOutputContext.java' interface offers methods for navigating and writing key-value pairs within MapReduce tasks, inheriting from `TaskAttemptContext`. 'TaskReport.java' encapsulates a task's execution state, including ID, progress, state, diagnostics, and counters, implementing the `Writable` interface for serialization. 'TaskTrackerInfo.java' represents TaskTracker information, implementing `Writable` for serialization, with fields like name, blacklist status, and reason. The 'counters' directory manages counters, using abstract classes like 'AbstractCounters' and 'FileSystemCounterGroup', adhering to the Abstract Factory and Bridge patterns. The 'filecache' directory's 'ClientDistributedCacheManager.java' manages job submission cache, determining file timestamps and visibility. The 'jobhistory' directory tracks job executions, with classes like 'AvroArrayUtils', 'JobSubmittedEvent', and 'MapAttemptFinishedEvent' ensuring comprehensive job history. The 'lib' directory provides utility classes for diverse MapReduce functionalities, following various architectural patterns. The 'protocol' directory's 'ClientProtocol.java' and 'ClientProtocolProvider.java' define communication protocol between JobClient and JobTracker, with the latter providing an abstract factory for protocol instances. The 'security' directory's 'JobTokenIdentifier.java' class extends TokenIdentifier, storing job IDs for secure token management. The 'server' directory's 'JTConfig.java' and 'TTConfig.java' define JobTracker and TaskTracker configuration parameters, respectively. The 'split' directory manages input split metadata, ensuring efficient job scheduling and task execution. The 'task/reduce' directory's components manage the reduce phase, including shuffle and merge operations. Lastly, 'MRJobConfUtil.java' offers utility methods for configuring MapReduce jobs, while 'LogParams.java' in the 'v2' directory manages log parameters for Hadoop MapReduce v2.
